description: Arabic-Hebrew True Cognate semantic disambiguation with ABC-randomized multiple choice

definitions.A: The Arabic sentence is semantically appropriate.
definitions.B: The Hebrew sentence is semantically appropriate.

undiac.name: True Cognate Disambiguation / undiac

diac.name: True Cognate Disambiguation / diac

uroman.name: True Cognate Disambiguation / uroman

ipa.name: True Cognate Disambiguation / IPA

undiac_uroman.name: True Cognate Disambiguation / undiac+uroman
undiac_uroman.template:
You are given two sentences: one in Arabic script and one in Hebrew script.
Each sentence is also provided in Uroman transliteration.

Which sentence is semantically appropriate?

A. {sent_a}
B. {sent_b}
C. Both are appropriate

Answer with only the letter (A/B/C).

undiac_ipa.name: True Cognate Disambiguation / undiac+IPA
undiac_ipa.template:
You are given two sentences: one in Arabic script and one in Hebrew script.
Each sentence is also provided in IPA transcription.

Which sentence is semantically appropriate?

A. {sent_a}
B. {sent_b}
C. Both are appropriate

Answer with only the letter (A/B/C).
